shortDescription.text: Identifies skills that are confirmed to be benign and provide legitimate functionality or guidance.

fullDescription.text: This rule flags skills that, after thorough analysis and reconciliation with external threat intelligence, are determined to be non-malicious. They typically describe standard development practices, provide informational guidance, or implement legitimate tools without any confirmed intent for exfiltration, exploitation, command and control, or persistence. While not a security vulnerability, it serves to confirm the benign nature of the analyzed skill.

message.text: Threat intelligence strongly refuted any suspicion of malicious intent. The skill describes a legitimate and well-structured guide for End-to-End (E2E) testing principles, failure taxonomy, and workflow, including standard CLI commands like 'yarn playwright test'. No suspicious lines were identified in the initial analysis. No confirmed malicious intent.

snippet.text: The exact source code for SKILL.md was not provided. The analysis indicates it is a markdown document outlining E2E testing principles and workflow, including identifying test configurations, executing standard testing commands, and applying specific remediation strategies based on failure categories.
